candidates.direction_name: Swap To The Single-K 128x128 Non-Microkernel Sibling
ranking_notes: This is the cleanest next experiment that directly targets the long-scoreboard gap exposed by the grouped-row result.
It preserves the accepted hot-band row split, so the measurement stays focused on one kernel-variant change.
candidates.hypothesis: The grouped-row experiment showed that launch-order changes alone are not enough. The existing single-K 128x128 sibling keeps the accepted row split and one-K stage depth while changing the accumulate ordering away from the microkernel-specific path, which may reduce long-scoreboard waste directly.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: microkernel-specific accumulate ordering is contributing to long-scoreboard stalls on the accepted hot-band split
candidates.risk: Moderate risk: the variant is in-tree, but it still lacks a clean measurement on the accepted row split.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if runtime regresses again while long-scoreboard stalls stay flat or worsen.
expected_local_changes: Restore the grouped-row constant back to the clean accepted base if needed.
Swap the default hot-band launch from the PTX microkernel path to the existing single-K non-microkernel 128x128 sibling.

candidates.direction_name: Retune PTX Launch Bounds On The Clean Baseline
ranking_notes: This remains worth keeping because the clean PTX path is still latency-limited.
It ranks behind the non-microkernel sibling because the grouped-row result points more strongly at scoreboard behavior than at occupancy.
candidates.hypothesis: The clean PTX hotspot still sits at 200 registers per thread with occupancy capped at two blocks per SM. Tightening __launch_bounds__ may trim enough register pressure to improve latency hiding if the compiler does not spill.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: register pressure and low CTA residency on the accepted PTX hot-band path
candidates.risk: High risk: forcing tighter launch bounds can introduce spills or a worse schedule, and the clean cuBLAS reference does not point to occupancy as the main missing ingredient.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop immediately if build output or NCU shows spills, or if runtime regresses without lowering register pressure.
expected_local_changes: Restore the grouped-row constant back to the clean accepted base if needed.
Retune the PTX microkernel launch-bounds annotation and keep the rest of the launch topology unchanged for the first measurement.

candidates.direction_name: Try A Shallower PTX Grouped-Row Setting
ranking_notes: This stays in reserve as the opposite-direction launch-order test after the 8-row setting failed.
It ranks third because the non-microkernel sibling is now the more direct scoreboard experiment.
candidates.hypothesis: The failed 8-row grouping suggests the PTX hot-band path may prefer tighter A locality over deeper B reuse. Dropping kFixedHotBandPtxGroupedRows from 4 to 2 tests the opposite launch-order direction without changing the row split or K-stage structure.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: current grouped-row traversal may still be mismatched to A/B locality balance on the accepted PTX hot-band path
candidates.risk: Moderate risk: this is the same family that just failed in the opposite direction, so the upside may be small.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if runtime change is within noise or if scoreboard stalls do not improve after one measurement.
expected_local_changes: Restore the grouped-row constant back to the clean accepted base if needed.
Decrease kFixedHotBandPtxGroupedRows from 4 to 2 and remeasure.

notes: Projected exactly three node_b directions into normalized search candidates.
